exemplar.title: Elderly Accessories - Improving the Quality of Life for the Elderly

detailedExemplarData.title: Elderly Accessories - Improving the Quality of Life for the Elderly

strengths: Comprehensive Value & Focus table that demonstrates clear media literacy and research skills, explaining how each method advanced the learning goal
Detailed descriptions of how literature reviews, media reviews, interviews, product analyses, and surveys were usedâproviding strong evidence of ATL skill application
Focus group synthesis (Figure 9) presented findings clearly and shows effective collaboration and information-organizing skills
Color-coded action plan illustrates strong self-management skills in product creation
Use of LINE messages to gather expert input showcases adaptability and active listening in unconventional contexts
Reflection on applying the Design Cycle demonstrates transfer of skills to new contexts
Structured focus group analysis with diverse participant profiles highlights robust public-speaking and social skills
weaknesses: The literature review schedule is well organized but lacks summarized key insights or findings under each entry to fully demonstrate analytical research skills
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The work excels in describing and evidencing how ATL skills were applied both to the learning goal and the product, with multiple concrete examples spanning research, collaboration, self-management and transfer of learning. To push this section into the highest band, the student should include succinct summaries of key findings in the literature review schedule to showcase deeper analytical engagement.

strengths: Clear articulation of the product goal with well-justified success criteria (safety, function, practicality, cost) grounded in research insights
A detailed rubric in Figure 2 linking each success criterion to a justification and specific test methods
Integration of success criteria into an action plan with color-coding and status dropdowns, demonstrating thoughtful sequencing and resource planning
weaknesses: Learning goal description remains broad and would benefit from being broken into SMART objectives
Lack of explicit, measurable indicators of progress for the learning goal (e.g., number of interviews, volume of literature reviewed)
Mind-map covers too many ideas; narrowing to one or two high-impact concepts would streamline planning and ensure feasibility
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Overall, the planning shows a high level of organization and feasibility through a detailed rubric and action plan. The product goal is grounded in research and sequenced effectively. To further strengthen this section, the learning goal needs more specificity through SMART objectives and measurable success criteria, and the ideation phase could be focused more narrowly for clarity.

strengths: Deep self-reflection on personal growth as an inquirer and designer, showing strong insight into the impact on learning
Sophisticated awareness of communication challenges across three languages and their effect on project outcomes
Forward-looking reflection linking project strategies to future Diploma Programme demands
Thoughtful, criterion-by-criterion evaluation of the productâs performance with clear plans for enhancement
Concluding synthesis of personal growth, passion, and future aspirations, demonstrating a mature understanding of the projectâs broader impact
weaknesses: Self-evaluation rubric ratings would be more convincing if supported by direct user feedback or excerpts from survey data to validate each score
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Reflection is a standout feature, offering detailed and forward-focused insights into how the project shaped the studentâs skills and mindset. The criterion evaluations are thorough, but anchoring the self-assessment with actual user feedback or data would lend even greater credibility to the conclusions.

annotations.comment: Your learning goal description is detailed but remains broad. Break it into SMART objectives (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound) to strengthen your planning.

Your Value & Focus table for research methods demonstrates detailed media literacy skills. You explain how each method contributed to your inquiries, showing effective application of ATL research skills (B1).

You clearly describe how literature reviews, media reviews, interviews, product analyses, and surveys each advanced your learning goal. This depth of evidence supports top-band ATL skill application (B1).

Your learning goal is clearly rooted in personal experience, but it lacks explicit, measurable success criteria. Define specific indicators of progress (e.g., number of interviews conducted, literature reviewed) to make your goal assessable.

You present a clear product goal and justify the choice of criteria (safety, function, practicality, cost) with research insights. This aligns well with detailed planning for your product (Strand II).
Your detailed rubric in Figure 2 clearly links each success criterion to justification and test methods. This thorough structure demonstrates a high level of planning and feasibility (A2).

Integrating your success criteria into the action plan with color-coding and status dropdowns shows strong sequencing and resource planning. This approach supports a clear roadmap for product development (A2/A3).
Your mindmap is rich in ideas but covers a broad range of topics. Narrow your focus to one or two high-impact concepts to streamline planning and ensure feasibility.

Your focus group slide (Figure 9) synthesizes findings in a visually clear way, demonstrating strong collaboration and information-organizing skills. This supports effective application of social ATL skills (B1/B2).

Your action plan with color-coded criteria and status indicators is an excellent example of organization skills in product creation. This demonstrates clear application of self-management ATL skills (B2).
Using LINE messages to gather expert input illustrates adaptability and active listening in a non-traditional medium. This showcases strong social skill application (B1).
Reflecting on your use of the Design Cycle to create the solution highlights transfer skills, showing you can apply learning in new contexts (B2).
Your literature review schedule is well organized, but summarizing key insights or findings under each entry would strengthen evidence of your analytical research skills (B1).
Your structured focus group product analysis with diverse participant profiles demonstrates robust public-speaking and social skills in gathering user feedback (B2).
You provide deep self-reflection on how the project influenced your growth as an inquirer and designer, demonstrating strong personal insight into learning impacts (C1).
Reflecting on communication challenges across three languages shows sophisticated awareness of how interpersonal skills affect project outcomes (C1).
Linking project strategies to future Diploma Programme demands demonstrates effective transfer of learning and strong forward-looking reflection (C1).
Your self-evaluation rubric scores are logical, but they would be more convincing if supported by direct user feedback or survey data excerpts to validate each rating (C2).
Your evaluation of each criterionâincluding safety improvements and functionality insightsâshows thoughtful analysis and clear plans for further enhancement (C2).
Your concluding reflection effectively synthesizes personal growth, passion, and future aspirations, illustrating a mature understanding of the projectâs broader impact (C1).
